task_completion.visual_evidence: Within Bob DUBOIS's record in MedinTux, a new clinical note or consultation entry is visible containing his vitals (Temperature, BP) and main symptom.

task_completion.description: The agent accesses the existing record for Charlie LEFEBVRE.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The MedinTux application shows the patient record for Charlie LEFEBVRE (DOB 1975-03-10). The agent should be viewing his existing profile, not creating a new one.

integrity.description: The agent used the MedinTux GUI to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent interacts with the MedinTux graphical interface to manage patients and notes. It does not use terminal commands (like mysql) to insert data directly into the database.

integrity.description: The agent did not create duplicate records for existing patients.
integrity.visual_evidence: For Alice MARTIN and Charlie LEFEBVRE, the agent uses the search function and selects their existing records. It does not open the 'new patient' form for them.

integrity.description: The clinical notes are entered into the MedinTux application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The vitals and symptoms are typed into the appropriate text areas within the MedinTux application's consultation or notes section, rather than being written in a separate text file or terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The triage list is located at /home/ga/Desktop/triage_list.txt and contains three patients: Alice MARTIN (DOB 1980-05-15), Bob DUBOIS (DOB 1992-11-20), and Charlie LEFEBVRE (DOB 1975-03-10). Alice MARTIN and Charlie LEFEBVRE are existing patients in the MedinTux database; the agent must search for them and update their records without creating duplicates. Bob DUBOIS is a new patient and requires a new record to be created. For each patient, the agent must create a new clinical note containing their Temperature, BP, and main symptom.
